textByHash.163fb7635afaf35282fa7076ad26b2079b136fcd: Well all that politics downstairs was getting a bit dry, but then I heard there'd been a bit of a commotion. I thought I'd come see what the fuss was all about. You don't mind, do you?
textByHash.d8c9ed44e79ee7a416254f60db1a91fe7ac71d96: Wonderful! And you! Who are you?
textByHash.961f3ec232ea3190f891133702ec67a1252a476b: Kuaini! Pleased to meet you. Crown Prince Itzla Arkan, heir to the Sun Throne, at your service.
textByHash.e2427afd0b4e45cb3379e4b7bf8e01abbed2a55e: Tell me, , are you the one who caught these would-be assassins?
textByHash.5c61f43bc687d4d37db7227c880635a79060be35: Well then, why don't you and I ask this one a few little questions. You don't mind, do you, sergeant?
textByHash.d4d0469ffdb3d0a71e2f6328aaadccd20c6e361b: Nilsal to you, iknami. I hear you managed to get yourself into a spot of bother.
textByHash.7af880881785b5fffd61e68ebeb84543f127d6f2: Ah, tetamo! Such a shame. No matter though! We'll have you talking in no time.
textByHash.66847c7889f207e04f8b4e8a8029674b2d816976: Now, you'll need to forgive me, but I'm a bit new around here, so your customs are not familiar to me. Tell me, iknami, what's the preferred method of interrogation in these parts?
textByHash.609df442c59e7c8b1a6fae0e0543c1a0e91c2297: Well it just seemed polite to ask. We have all sorts of fun methods back home in Varlamore. We could give one or two of them a go if you want?
textByHash.962d8ec4440765941caf3a8a9208682d9ecf5cb7: Absolutely! Now, what are your thoughts on chicken?
textByHash.23734d3e46b7bee96a48a5b7f7c62f0443269b17: Oh, , do you not have chickens here? Sorry, again, all this is quite new to me.
textByHash.c17158d7c62adaeb8a441289c2403f3d8b901751: Well then, why the confusion? Have you never seen one before? Oh you poor sheltered thing. Did your parents never show you the wonders of the countryside?
textByHash.e4d941b6d9725305719cc85ca0b5416871fd5cc8: Well why didn't you say so?
textByHash.a2729cdda11dff56b50828bd4a9237cfb684ee94: No game at all, iknami. The reason I ask is because one of our preferred methods in Varlamore involves just a small amount of fire, and you'd never believe it, but the smell of human flesh once it gets going...
textByHash.d61d1bbdba83ddfdb79558c9dde826d18e516e53: Cook? No, not at all! That would imply I'm going to eat you, and I'm not a barbarian! Though, it would be interesting to know if it tastes like chicken as well...
textByHash.670dfca806e1e7466c4b093a517f966ffa90c675: So who are you working for?
textByHash.fe45dcf5ca60be28893ab966a5ea42efa6234369: Why did they want you to attack the delegation?
textByHash.5d4b9cbbd6cff7ed3f06f3a9caffdfdb55284675: So a Varlamorian paid you to assassinate the Teokan? You're not giving me much to go on.
textByHash.fe951ffd0317aee53214beaeaef59b5555680a5c: Well I think we're done here. Come.
textByHash.aa3cc5d1f83e55769863f019c2da5c081deba8ea: I think we got what we needed there.
textByHash.04e1ab64060459631ff0ad724ff2267b9423270c: Oh yes. The fear of being cooked will do that to a person. All total rubbish of course! Imagine burning someone alive! We're much more civilised over in Varlamore.
textByHash.dc924329e27a83ea51b0ed0f4064a55a2c313234: We just cut out their heart while it's still beating instead.
textByHash.81333a8483dd5822c8657c2a4e298f048725f17b: Anyway, sounds like there'll be some work to do when we get back home. An assassination attempt on the Teokan of Ralos... Concerning stuff. First up though, I'd better get back to the politics!
textByHash.16f8e242a8b14a4f03bd19f76b50339692e0f025: , once we've got these papers signed, you lot will be free to travel to Varlamore for the first time! No more needing explicit permission. You can come and go as you please!
textByHash.470143e9963af2a07eea9e2595873d843e913cef: Now, it's entirely up to you of course, but given we have you to thank for stopping this plot, I'd be very keen if you'd join me in Varlamore to continue the investigation.
textByHash.1d12032bdebadd941e744cec9f19c7c4614400f4: No need to decide now. It will take a bit longer for us to be ready for visitors anyway, but when the time comes, I'll have my people send word to you.
textByHash.86bbc3db954638f3ac6327c96416726495816480: No need to decide now, but if you do wish to accept my offer, just speak to Regulus Cento outside the eastern gates of this city.
textByHash.4e5bbb31344d105cd080e881a72de82ed501a7a5: Now, they'll be missing me downstairs. Timoiva, and may the sun light your way!
textByHash.900b00b956a2ffd601f283630bab4546b19958cb: You love it, don't you? Standing on a stage spouting nonsense to a bunch of fanatics! It means nothing, Metzli!
textByHash.5aedd66a4ae918fbb220507d5ed17ff2b19fa264: You want my blood? Take it! Just make sure you take it all! At least that way I won't have to listen to the garbage you spew any more!
textByHash.6bcf3cdf74bfb1e14f3972a93ea009b43d14278e: I heard Metzli got away. I'm here to help.
textByHash.753e1df48d90ddcc515df3cd0e072d145db9722f: Mother... This is my burden to bear. Let me finish this.
textByHash.0f38a8f46c86a0652d9fd8e9132e6b79cce4db72: You were going to go yourself, even though they need you up here. Why you and not me?
textByHash.387897d9f497bf0edae8850c35f5f1874cb939b7: Nagua! I'll deal with them. You go on ahead. Don't worry, I'll catch up.
textByHash.fbc3ad78ba3d0d09c0a2eb6e07386b06fc9f975c: Can you believe this place was down here all this time? We had no idea!
textByHash.f0957da4e3cc9a93b6fd18f2cb8de02f214dff7d: Well... sort of, I suppose. We always knew the crypt was originally built by the Old Ones, but we had no idea how far all of this stretched. It's not like anyone was ever allowed to come down here and check!
textByHash.9b55ad1f36e228a3e2d0d044b2d9debacd46516d: Anyway, there hasn't been any sign of Metzli or Furia, but they must have come this way. Let's have a look around and see if we can find a way forward.
textByHash.67bd1b2468de2febf2caad5eea1d8ccc5451cc31: Well, we seem to have more of these strange teleport tiles here... Maybe we should see where they lead?
textByHash.741a40cf626128811e364b086aaa81f0a2bd611f: We're making progress. Let's keep it up.
textByHash.fb7f8ca264ac4d54ed8c1a3fb98ea941260dfb63: Well, we managed to activate a statue in one of these rooms. My guess is we now need to activate the one in the other.
textByHash.1c402d6b5d760047dcbc766330b37c43f7e156d2: Well, we've got another one of those statues, and these altars around the edges also look important. The layout of the room seems quite specific as well. It's almost like a compass, or maybe even a clock!
textByHash.9b2c8e03add62b7e86b2e9448f4943086e89f351: True, but maybe the Old Ones used a different kind of clock to us...
textByHash.29a0a35eb6def95edb277ae505f8a0e5a6676641: Well, this thing we're doing with the altars seems to be working, so I say we just keep doing that.
textByHash.7259956b1c3277e9d128901838f5b7bbceefa3a8: Of course, iknami! What do you need translating?
textByHash.886e57c33687937952834bfc6c35e1dbb1d30cf3: Oma? I think that means two.
textByHash.7707502321828e30d56d1d24b134166c1cb30c63: Naui? This is the one I struggle with. It might mean four.
textByHash.a57da22f1f085915ae9033227d8bf6f3952650e2: Kuli? I think that means five.
textByHash.3b932541c8b7320cd315b3dbda1261991d803ca9: Chaki? I think that means six.
textByHash.28185682db6fc8c1ed54d1fe3e27519e5315d6aa: Koma? I think that means seven.
textByHash.e8537f8a8d58cb468efa77d6ca46f692b9e16358: Ueai? I think that means eight.
textByHash.be5901c5b6b98753a67fe25e98fd6c243b92813c: Makti? I think that means ten.
textByHash.9b9cf0e6a54bb5d5b54b508918d54e0c8244fb5f: Hmm... I'm not too sure on that one.
textByHash.fcaef5517dddf20792f0095068418f36d26da4e7: Hopefully it helps in some way, iknami.
textByHash.37fb7edcb96f756cbfc4ee28b410795cd437c525: Hmm... It looks similar to the altars around the room. Where did you find it?
textByHash.583eab8cbfe75f61e701704b83472c30a4978614: Well, I doubt it was left here by accident...
textByHash.406d195cb04eb05a1ab514e30957d6c574a674d5: Ah, the teleporter seems to have separated us.
textByHash.d6437f5a81ce76bbb24a8535c61708a0acaf2f07: Well, I might still be able to be of use from here. These look like pressure plates. I might be able to use them to help you in some way.
textByHash.e527dc6b14e212af00bff8e8ed2731b2e80c489c: I'm not really sure. Those braziers around the room look like they could be important though.
textByHash.14c524913628eb721e8056153810bae4f27d7eb3: Well it seems I can use these pressure plates to help you get past the fires. Beyond that, I'm not too sure. Those braziers around the room look like they could be important though.
textByHash.4cec2170ec3a9b42e427e0da1b874dfb19c9e9fe: We seem to be making progress. Keep it up.
textByHash.a4bea10b7f1278cfa90fcff0d36e74468a7f7d9e: Nice work! I think you did it!
textByHash.005700dc9531e61d5c3aecc04d0a71d7c212415a: If all goes well, we find Metzli and Furia and we show them the error of their ways. No doubt they won't be willing to hear reason, so I suspect it will get a touch violent in the process...
textByHash.416dcda1486f4bb277c91de6e80ed637babeaffd: But then, once that's done, maybe we go and have a nice drink somewhere. I know I need one.
textByHash.58e4abd6a18985b8d7067a2b74b4697b2e2b9198: Hmm... Well, I guess there's only one way to find out... Shall we?
textByHash.536957c58f2ec273603e1ece4e9b9181aa9c8b95: They can't be too far ahead now. Let's find them.
textByHash.c904cd28810fa5595b4d82f1fccb68c5e276ed1f: This ends here, Metzli. No more running.
textByHash.2be4b15bc3657a5f7563b1bea4b96e077d4df1ac: And where's that exactly? Another Old One ruin? That's all this place is. There's no centre of the world here. Face it, Metzli, you've failed. Your Final Dawn has failed.
textByHash.ce188386d1d731889b335ae45947112e6230d0ce: She's injured. She won't be going far. Let's get after her.
textByHash.d72eef59070cd7cc5ebfeec64521ba55269c40ff: Metzli won't get far now she's injured. Let's find her.
textByHash.9908502e37d140731283c3b444c6fb316f096585: Of course there's nothing here, Metzli. What were you expecting? Did you think Ralos himself was going to pop out and share the secrets of the world with you?
textByHash.5ea1e1ff3d0acd789b26b117e67300262b2fe252: And yet it's empty. Perhaps this 'gift' was never meant for us. Or maybe it never existed at all. Either way, there's no 'truth' to be found here. Looks like we'll just have to keep making our own.
textByHash.79142a6458ea950db9990768bce675509f08c35e: Well... I suppose that's that then.
textByHash.d698b8f6643c5d39a2e42c6edfa77e1dc342b6a9: We'd better take Furia here to my mother. She'll be the one who decides what happens to her.
textByHash.7e5ed8f4fd347ef1330c2fba75b29038ff8eb394: Furia, come with us, and don't even think about trying anything.
textByHash.d02f9d42b89d4f3966f2ff78d6d10f702cf3c3b5: Fair enough. Don't take too long.
textByHash.3913c7318bd5fecd3531509372df85b6aada258b: It's done. Metzli is dead. This one here is all that remains.
textByHash.341d0c4f38c6fea83773c0c7af4d84504a6a9cd1: If I may, mother... Metzli and Ennius are already dead. The Emissaries are now without leaders and are unlikely to be much of a threat.
textByHash.85f7b06c8c56d6f695fc8a568c3dbeaac46dc008: But there are still many out there who supported them. As far as they're concerned, we've already made two martyrs today. It might be prudent for us to keep Furia alive.
textByHash.7b88cff3ccf3687a59b1c6ebf99e3f5855a08895: Well, that was quite the adventure.
textByHash.335489e0f4fa083f5162154a3cfb4c27a14ef8dd: Tetamo! I was working very hard, thank you very much. I was conducting essential research on our enemy from the last place they'd expect!
textByHash.a53de4b5b45078bb6f836334c41fe1febbda50cf: Indeed I did, iknami. Speaking of which, did you discover anything of note down in the ruins?
textByHash.91b5fcdebcbc810a4d91a65ff9964c5783d6857f: Interesting... All this time we've thought of Mokhaiotl as the centre of the world, and yet it was something else entirely. Makes you wonder what else it is that we don't know...
textByHash.b2a8d9822f76483ff4142497453084b032416015: Still, I guess it doesn't really matter. Metzli fooled herself into thinking that she could find all the answers, yet the beauty of this world is that we are free to look upon it as we desire.
textByHash.68934b7521675baab6352cf33e37451acf8ee0bb: But enough about Metzli. I have something far more important to talk about.
textByHash.d56ee816b03c7d1e8b0dca6a66a558d04fc898dd: While I know you of course needed no convincing to put your life at risk to rescue me, it feels only right to show my thanks by giving you a little something.
textByHash.2d22fd96a2a88cb6926a88e2bb55a68653b7b44e: Ah, but I see you don't have space for it right now. Come back when you do.
textByHash.64b1cfaba77a109da6cae2b44880ead065d4ab2f: This blade once belonged to my grandfather, but I believe its path now lies with you.
textByHash.662e4f34529116ff9fc4091a2d35f10244335f7c: And I can't think of anyone more deserving to wield it, iknami. Now, I'd best return to the palace. Timoiva, my friend, and may the sun light your way.
textByHash.1099b6b8e90c6cef8f83526f28ca67aa737aa811: Nilsal. As I was saying, it feels only right for me to show my thanks to you by giving you a little something.
textByHash.d335db73cfe94f0539f52f20fd6efa1349ab8e0c: Well, now that the local cult of murderers is no longer trying to kill me or my father, I'd say things are looking up.
textByHash.dcc1541b5cd856cf0eda955d32508fe8aac6cb6f: I'm also finding I've got a bit more time on my hands now that things are all sorted. I suggested to mother I spend that time in the Colosseum. I don't know if she was totally convinced.
textByHash.916ecaf1d2e5a975776d6cdc35150012023b622e: Anyway, I won't keep you, iknami. I'm sure you've got plenty more adventures to go on. Timoiva, my friend.
textByHash.60287b1f585f6df3b4eba0b57ebaff9666999c62: Well, iknami, as luck would have it, someone came along trying to sell a sword that perfectly matches the one I gave you. He claimed he just found it on the ground!
textByHash.28b694411a2c9a7cc4a3d141fa96a33f50382aab: Indeed. I'm sure it can't be the same one. After all, that was a one of a kind family heirloom, so you'd definitely have taken good care of it.
textByHash.7379098173c9a5d24d79044b6f3fd6a866e5dc3d: Anyway, I had to pay 250,000 coins to buy this one, so if you want it, that's how much I'll have to charge you.
textByHash.f34ad54cb9aef11ad7d418b9013de36a49c42ea2: I'm afraid not, iknami. You'll have to go and find where you left the one I gave you. I'm sure you won't have lost it. After it, it's a one of a kind family heirloom.
textByHash.566f61dbbb7b0ba16b38fbf9b7af7bd924afb26e: Father should make a full recovery, though given that his would-be killers are still out there, that's not anywhere near as much comfort as it should be.
textByHash.8e258e7c2dd1e2245de41d07bae423b8f8a53fa6: I've sent word to mother about what happened here, but I've not heard back yet. Once I have, we can start planning our next move.
textByHash.b0fd9cf5c56fc01d576c9e71b721b0fda951537e: I've sent word to mother about what happened here, but I've not heard back yet. However, in the meantime, I've devised a little plan for how we might go about dealing with our cultist problem.
textByHash.ccbb25515c73c7c6829a2593feb65058307c7bc6: The Twilight Emissaries of course! If they are the ones responsible for what is now two attempts on my father's life, their deaths will be swift and brutal.
textByHash.6c725fc2192d369649741e025212d6be6f732ec1: However, given we're somewhat civilised people, we can't exactly start executing cultists without definitive proof. How do we get that? By joining up of course!
textByHash.3c943271788e527fcc9dacd09446ed44f5be8388: Exactly! Quite a clever plan, if I do say so myself.
textByHash.32b1ddd23d869498f30ec7bc58cb00ebf7f670b7: Very well. I'll be here when you're ready.
textByHash.e5c1372b38380700936d4854d6643f1eb2a99513: He wished to return to the Teomat. I advised against it, but...
textByHash.23fef3dbf6f93c5fdb0aa43985405358d35513ce: With respect, progress has been made. We believe the Twilight Emissaries to be responsible, but we need to obtain proof before we can act.
textByHash.fdcc346cedc6b7c9adb5aca70efc0335fa22611c: We will infiltrate the cult and gather any information we can. Once we have confirmed their guilt, they will be eradicated.
textByHash.5c017064ce9bc0504eedb1b50bede2735320c491: This is a task of upmost importance. I intend to lead the infiltration myself.
textByHash.5b713dd6e2450e76f84d86afea4938e73f1c83dd: In Varlamore we lead from the front!
textByHash.156330c22827a4a2162dd9b5a19fd7f66f3eec74: I cannot trust them. We have been infiltrated and our enemies now sit beside us.
textByHash.7897d1f00986432a94403e73acfff6b5e698a0d5: It could be more than just her. One of my knights was implicated, but another may have incriminated him.
textByHash.be3e65537c098c46edb7d9239b2368d2f581a76e: There have been suspicions directed at Furia and Ennius.
textByHash.4bf9ae4874fa77c51cfff6f1642a5f5f74a016e5: And if there is no one I can trust?
textByHash.092ea5a2853bc84966a9f16bd1863a5240c6f31e: Yes, my Queen. May the sun light your way.
textByHash.7aa9f86bed8d5bbfca50a3d85e51845352035fb4: It would seem I won't be able to join you on this mission, iknami. Still, I have no doubt you will be successful all the same.
textByHash.350fae50874ffc1655bce0ea49b581a32a03bb5b: Head to the pub in Quetzacalli Gorge and ask to rent the basement room. I have a contact who will meet you there.
textByHash.54aad356be0f4334f9e4f25b345ef3aab1422a8f: One of the only people left that I can trust. They'll fill you in on the details of how you'll infiltrate the Emissaries. Good luck. May the sun light your way.
textByHash.22df0f2b98cde84e872a3b5f18c20899fceac394: Nilsal. You should head on over to the pub in Quetzacalli Gorge. Remember, you'll need to rent the basement room. My contact will meet you there.
textByHash.3c2633b7c131137f92c1203f3d321d0faefa2ea6: Isn't it obvious? I'm your contact!
textByHash.a14e3e08371e82b1987ccb62ae7050077b0c2920: Look, I fully appreciate what my mother said. I have carefully considered her instructions, but in this one very specific situation, I have come to the conclusion that they must be disregarded.
textByHash.739c371c937394cfa9ed52a81126f56633b755b3: Well that's why I've covered my face of course. Simple!
textByHash.94db1ae02d1ca3f7b41863cef05137214dc3398d: You may well be right, iknami, but that is a bridge we will cross, and then quite possibly burn, when we get to it.
textByHash.c6f5676d25f35177cee4160ce7768a32db056b60: I can see you're still not convinced, so let me be frank. I wasn't lying when I spoke to mother. The number of people we can trust is concerningly low.
textByHash.ba54399ce9b978584d15920990001bd094a39b34: Metzli, one of the most important people in the entire kingdom, has seemingly betrayed us. If your suspicions are right, Furia and Ennius are guilty as well. For all we know, there could be even more.
textByHash.bcd695c6b85ce43ffde93dbc71cd4ee60eb6e4a3: At this point, the fewer people who know about our mission, the better.
textByHash.07aea1c84718923568fa7db90d3ea8cb81f0b2a5: The irony is, my friend, that as an outsider, you're actually far more trustworthy than any Varlamorian now. You almost certainly can't be part of this conspiracy.
textByHash.eeb63d3b394f8a5cb7df8405730cb8721f8f7129: We need to infiltrate the Twilight Emissaries and confirm their guilt. I've done some digging, and rumour has it that they're on a bit of a recruitment spree at the moment. That's our way in.
textByHash.0eb66392c61c146eec105ba94d33ac9ac18334d5: The cult makes their home in the Twilight Temple, east of here. I suspect they won't take kindly to us just walking in, but the local shopkeeper here might be able to help us out.
textByHash.f3fd9c3ca0c0c2d4e002579770372b96c4f755e7: He's been engaging in many a charitable undertaking recently. Food for the poor, potions for the sick, that sort of thing. You'd be mistaken for thinking him to actually be a reasonable person.
textByHash.24c93c4b5ae9705148621680e5cb7c4026e0c7c8: Sadly not in this case. The Emissaries accept members from all walks of life, but the vast majority of their recruits are those from more unfortunate backgrounds.
textByHash.26af3a2ad5ea622e9351b1880adf4465eed2a033: Exactly. It is... unfortunate. The War of Betrayal was over a lifetime ago, but many still feel the impacts, especially in this part of the kingdom.
textByHash.54de43df70878085f491402a588609c1a4b97e8e: I'd like to think we have made great improvements over the years, but this is a clear reminder that far more needs to be done.
textByHash.0204cbc9e142366cb27c894dd26fc3cbd053d953: Anyway, it's not something to dwell on now. Let us get back to the more pressing matter of joining a dangerous cult.
textByHash.5146d92df473652f800ab5c9332ddb3ec5ce0434: Exactly! Return to me here once you've hopefully been successful and we can discuss the next steps.
textByHash.d7e03d8feaedf78861f3d4e2cd9e67f736a5d216: That's parents for you. Do as I say, but not as I do. She was never afraid to get her own hands dirty. Did you know that before she was even my age, she became a champion in the Colosseum?
textByHash.fd2afb1ac6ac4e76f7e6b1921d1f6fe8a4cb783c: I suspect you are right, but I would prefer to learn from my own mistakes. Every ruler must forge their own path. The people will never follow a leader who cannot stand alone.
textByHash.aca4425803586e0e6b0bc370f1dc80d50336950a: Fear not, iknami. I am confident that you will probably survive this mission.
textByHash.d94878aa8c87e35decd057b82745379dadbb9521: Metzli has always been an odd one. Everyone was always quick to dismiss it as just a little side effect of spending a lot of time with dead people. Now I wonder if she used that to her advantage.
textByHash.c9990fa46de2fe9a96c7bef9d82ba1778c879038: No one cared to pay close attention to her. No one wanted to spend much time around her. She probably got away with all sorts of things without anyone even noticing.
textByHash.a85cbfc5d4ffca37ce3c64838ebc991a2042fb21: The evidence seems to suggest so. Why else would she have disappeared after the attack?
textByHash.e6bca33546d11d6f0d2bc429bfc06d97db6aea17: Maybe, but surely someone would have noticed. People generally don't go quietly when captured.
textByHash.3762c60f666954a4a96e4e72abde87a9260a84cc: Well their family has of course produced traitors before, but they've never seemed to have much love for old Imafore.
textByHash.58b30b28dcb9a0319ae6a1e93e6f676021106cc8: By the end of the war, I think he'd ended up killing more of his own family members than he left alive. Amazing what paranoia will do to a man.
textByHash.708735d75bb7cfdcb92a568dd2b716e5d9f3d571: Still, even if they never cared for him, his blood still runs in their veins, and they've never been short on ambition themselves.
textByHash.c0b502ccfb8b96be621b7238b9e2160cd85333a5: The same thing we do to all traitors in Varlamore.
textByHash.9f5972a03e6d5b0c9d7250b6268db36e91c16892: He's in the capital now, where I wanted him to be from the start. He should be much safer there.
textByHash.7f21a869cbe49b40dbe8c93dafdf7365372e953b: Still, safer is not safe, especially if we do have traitors in our midst. At any moment we risk someone showing some basic competence and actually managing to kill him properly.
textByHash.6384cace08f990667f43bbcf47ff0f5d3d1d6db4: All I'm saying is that if I'd have been the one trying to assassinate my father, I'd probably have done a half- decent job.
textByHash.37c5fb86ddfd7ae16721fbf46336413be3fead18: So you challenge me, iknami? I'm wounded. However, as I said before, I do not feel we can trust many people while we have potential traitors around us.
textByHash.f0220d516a3f3857332d34deb56cd91f906a6573: We do not need to do this dance. I understand that where you come from, there is a certain art to holding a conversation. We do not bother with that here. You may be direct.
textByHash.74b33f3579870eb2b616da1976aaba89d598f4dd: I see. I understand your doubts, my friend. I suppose I have a few reasons for wanting to be here.
textByHash.a83bb38888889cef4025c9d95e23c6bbe5286f87: First, I do truly believe this mission has the highest chance of success if fewer people know about it. While I am not convinced on the competence of our foe, I still do not intend to underestimate them.
textByHash.3da7f15096621c246bd626ae7414ba331dcb0fa6: Second, I suppose I feel a certain level of responsibility. My father was attacked on my watch. When someone falters, it is their duty to fix it, especially when they hold a position like mine.
textByHash.7e7212c6aa34a30d94f8e477b2b69875cd3126be: After all, a leader who sits by and lets others deal with his mistakes is not someone to have faith in.
textByHash.b3c0c270af41f34f11787022c3023b661d73be3a: Finally, Servius is my father. Our relationship is not a simple one, but he is my father all the same. I would see to it personally that his attackers face justice.
textByHash.8509772f73a063bb9c8394b195e3cf5aca1e9f47: I hope that satisfies you, iknami.
textByHash.561a7c419cd103d057d0ea82e9cc6162612580a2: Nilsal. Any luck with that shopkeeper?
textByHash.3f79f7e6d4b709f72a1fceab25907bbd8c94022c: Keep at it. He's our only way of infiltrating the Emissaries.
textByHash.7d839ffdcd9d2686d069e36e23e224b65eee56d8: Kuani! Let us not waste any time then. I will meet you at the Twilight Temple. You'll find it east of here, on the other side of the Proudspire.
textByHash.ad0a97b5cb2a2be700301e2d46931c1d2b1df3b3: It seems this tower is where the Emissaries evaluate possible new members. If we're to get anywhere here, we're going to need to pass whatever tests they might have for us.
textByHash.bc5a87cc0f56b3a38dcdc63d5b4806bf5803fe12: Apparently, someone called Forebearer Janus will call upon us when it's our turn to be tested. In the meantime, we may as well mingle with these other potential recruits.
textByHash.cc7c956fd890f66d0bdf4d813074de3e07a641e9: Unlikely, but it would be interesting to know why they woke up one day and decided their calling in life was to join a cult.
textByHash.ef2a171acab542ff8339340d30eeab2b5cffd44f: Yes, I must admit the Emissaries have a more elaborate setup than expected. They've never been this organised before, nor have their numbers been so great. Something must have changed.
textByHash.8812213ff2ec3968f859df1cb0cedaddb6544e9d: Thankfully they are not quite that well put together. The temple here was built not long before the War of Betrayal. It is said that Imafore wanted somewhere dedicated to Ranul that would rival the Teomat.
textByHash.2dddc9a8a9b7c6d4f7a05d23645a06e13b36f992: I'm guessing he was too foolish to realise that the Teomat is equally dedicated to both Ralos and Ranul.
textByHash.ab90bd148236790b8b3ce9be3dec6cd987cb34f2: Anyway, the temple was considered an affront to both the Church of Ralos and the Sect of Ranul. After Imafore died, most people were more than happy to see it abandoned.
textByHash.2e5de0316c31c6229d7a3da30f38f003c749aec6: If all goes well, we will hopefully soon find out.
textByHash.5ecbdf28d656e0df3b795f2c0c228987e0d19c25: We still haven't been called upon yet. We may as well mingle with these other potential recruits while we wait.
textByHash.28342f000d570be2706f3a7e27a76b73511f30ef: Hmm... Did you learn anything else of note?
textByHash.9ff8a12ad9f778005b3afcb4af268d32cdccdde7: Mokhaiotl? I'm not my father, so you'll have to forgive my knowledge on this sort of thing, but if I remember right, Mokhaiotl plays a key role in Ximoua.
textByHash.b7cea270274558609a806ccc2dacd396309138cb: It is said that one day, Ralos and Ranul will come together, uniting all souls as one. In doing so, they will bring about the destruction of this world, but also the birth of a new one.
textByHash.b67ea096df9ae63a81c28300a2e232c6659c3dad: Most accept that we cannot predict when this day will come. However, our friends in the Twilight Emissaries don't seem happy with that. They instead believe in something called Tsak Tauli, the Final Dawn.
textByHash.2025c73cb0f1b70ee488866c9eef875f542deee5: When Ralos and Ranul come together, it is said they will do so at Mokhaiotl, the centre of the world.
textByHash.f7ba9c5f847e07eeea4357043c9a562ad60ec91d: Well I'd love to know how they plan on reaching the centre of the world. I can't imagine it's the kind of place that you can just stroll on down to.
textByHash.9073fdb89a2c43948bdcbb348c01de60630ad1f7: But it seems we will have to ponder this mystery later. Pick up your follower and then let us head inside and see what tests await us.
textByHash.92e34f7b661841ae8b9ce5fb8a65d176afe047cc: But it seems we will have to ponder this mystery later. Let us head in and see what tests await us.
textByHash.0d22439c3dfa0f03af02a05e7b382138d01ac5d8: It is said that Ralos and Ranul will come together at Mokhaiotl, the centre of the world. It is there that they will unite all souls as one, destroying this world and creating a new one.
textByHash.6cb73cf476641f37db8d6b642c6a3b0c27393b8e: These emissaries here are acting a bit strange... They keep putting their hands in their pockets.
textByHash.c5ffd6c1682ef1614756e1b597b031135f76b418: We should keep looking for that passphrase.
textByHash.a1793e54d617972209642a9c4f28912ad934cec8: Hmm... Seems some of the words are highlighted. I doubt that's a coincidence.
textByHash.b958ccc1510abaee0bc10e92c404b2b6c006ad37: Translate? Well I guess I can give it a go.
textByHash.4fb060e29e5c1f8fcde0ea2b2e38ebc82c5bdc3d: Ah, I recognise this language. It's the language of the Old Ones. Servius did teach me a few of their words, though I may not have paid as much attention as he'd have liked...
textByHash.a05f8f66bdd5be57d827981e237d54b3b872d03c: Uitt? If I remember correctly, that translates to 'south'.
textByHash.91d94de98889fda6338f9bc8ff7c8d02cc278b9c: Takam? If I remember correctly, that translates to 'east'.
textByHash.cce4f7b97b968f2ee2d81b8f5350b2373b6506d6: Well I hope it helps, iknami.
textByHash.5ff6175192e80e63268f6b8eee0865aa1e8917e1: Kuani! You'd better let Forebearer Janus know.
textByHash.491a604647ef2fff0bd2c4c6c7f2c6d5c86eb215: It seems some of the boxes have been left around the edges of the room...
textByHash.e81733b6005d459a9746e89054823fe04b0a47d8: I think it's time for a fight! This one should be fun!
textByHash.62ec5fc3ad2ca3aa96a2d2d46ae17310af394050: Great work there. I wonder what our next trial will be.
textByHash.9d4aa899b4f7d309666a7a67f8e6f74ca4cb5b7a: My guess is that one of them has some beliefs that don't quite line up with those of the Emissaries.
textByHash.2a53e9d29a5f0bf20ea0da64aa2aa2951082774e: They can't be truthful about something they don't know about. Take advantage of gaps in their knowledge - gaps that a true Emissary wouldn't have.
textByHash.bef54b885a2295124b7e5e9bbdc070f01dcaeadd: Shouldn't have joined a crazy murder cult then. Or if they really were committed to joining a crazy murder cult, they should have at least made sure they were actually good at it!
textByHash.54933bcaf6b16db4e06f0c636f63bd03a3fa1350: Probably not as much as we should, but what we do know is that the Emissaries believe in Tsak Tauli, more commonly known as the Final Dawn.
textByHash.e462eb6a4534eb665ebd7b5b15b9949c06fd9eaf: Like with Ximoua, the Final Dawn sees Ralos and Ranul come together, uniting all souls as one. In doing so they destroy this world and create a new one.
textByHash.feb295a0bab637e0fec94773ee60be99f7f0a9eb: After that, the two gods separate again, but the souls they united stay within them. The cycle then starts over once more.
textByHash.614ff03083abcfd71c604ccc229a803d91e2ddbb: The Emissaries seem to consider it their singular purpose to bring about the Final Dawn. Nothing else matters to them.
textByHash.068c3b951a37e0d6692d8404ebb6637a81dca5e0: Great work. On to the final trial!
textByHash.54f063fdf39f5c8ded6eb470691f1da402e78d11: Well, you heard the man. Shall we dance?
textByHash.6dfc68a0e693afff31c622456cd0e458adc530db: You might want to dodge this one!
textByHash.77b59a23b235b2cbcd7d4ad5c1d8a352376a04d6: I'd move if I were you!
textByHash.153d65fa252c1636eeb94754c00a33af02574e50: Don't be like that! Surely you must admit you enjoyed it a bit as well?
textByHash.9f6831061f0f7e7f1f76cf8c55d31d0ebccc0d8b: Right, moving swiftly on. Let's see what Forebearer Janus has to say to us.
textByHash.09281248c62e4adf7ecc80b48e302a5ef68915aa: We'd better change into those robes.
textByHash.95f7d0c8f7a8cc80fdc6694ca84cf1bd6b8613c8: Let's head over to the temple.
textByHash.2e73f9d3ae6df39bfee77d53ed4bf99a6c5cc41d: Well that was a level of insanity that even I wasn't ready for.
textByHash.53e530b5f4b50a1f6550ced011493e648b5ed774: Like I say, complete insanity! Metzli has twisted the minds of everyone here. They eagerly lap up her outlandish claims without ever stopping to consider how ludicrous they are.
textByHash.edfb31ecd5010027fd3fa5e2e7c8e917d97685fd: In some ways, I pity them, but alas, there is only one cure for this sort of thing. This cult must be eradicated, especially Metzli, Furia and Ennius.
textByHash.d18f8a78887a9a574065a25798d48101fff552ee: Hmm... A very good point. Could be this cult has just addled their brains, but it may be that they wanted us here.
textByHash.9723ab11d873712ddf082acb633ad1f699f0d43a: I guess we will find out soon enough.
textByHash.91824563955289f912b3953c9dd9a76f24eb7c8b: You said yourself, there are questions still unanswered. We must rectify that.
textByHash.1506f14a66569def98d27ca942800abbe0a101c9: Well it would be the last thing they'd expect.
textByHash.6db85dcbc4638ce7f6bdc060b387d21c8f5e0b23: Let's have a chat to this Forebearer Fides about our new duties. Whatever they are, hopefully they give us a chance to learn more.
textByHash.f8f8ca77c00f0940e584071db2f738a4a6e89d2e: Let's see what duties this Forebearer Fides has for us.
textByHash.a8078a67f704cab58ce7265dcfeb5bf3649c295b: Thank you! We're absolutely ecstatic to be here and we're ready to serve!
textByHash.ad8e4bb57f55eae39e5784dc38066082c2f97a92: And the Augur? She found those secrets below?
textByHash.dc8b1d1b636110069f73c0d3bf74ba4b5745d2ea: Let's head down into these ruins.
textByHash.3cfaeee1a7288011a5b18f9a718e21c7c125fc7e: How are you with a pickaxe, iknami? I must confess, my own skills with one are rather poor.
textByHash.675ac2eec798ffab74cc3142be819ff33081fbfe: We all have different callings, my friend. Mining just wasn't mine.
textByHash.bcfdab473c409e9f1cdc3c0c2fffcd346e5e9cd9: Oh I see! You win one fight and you start thinking you're something special.
textByHash.ce619b8d05c1c0e09ddcc2578eeb3b366ebf4d2d: Well then a bit of mining shouldn't be any issue for you then. Get to it!
textByHash.ab0191a2c17ddc88136d177b0456c2312aa9104e: Well... I'm alive, which I must be honest, I wasn't really expecting after that!
textByHash.bc1d6515793b7adfe241dceb6849233eb93a068d: Nor can I. I think we're just going to have to press on and hope that we can meet up again further ahead.
textByHash.cfebbacfc30d2483ecb47ffed34bbb1fd71e3ab8: Do you see any other options?
textByHash.28352133fd1c0e45e3f2900c6373d0f8f4e5cb49: Than I shall meet you up ahead. Good luck!
textByHash.fd659e4e755baccb91586aab33aabbeb6ce83864: There you are! I was starting to worry you'd got lost.
textByHash.c560436bb7cb831a93ff2f8f9981aee1845f8825: Well unfortunately it seems we have another before us. I can't see any way to open this door.
textByHash.43f908d2ed4f8e138f00f0f5fca52f738f2c172a: Oma? If I remember right, that means the number 'two'.
textByHash.c4c440735be267c0b2495c5fff983ec2d56e6d3f: I'm afraid I don't know what that one means.
textByHash.c104a13734a3e5d9846295e602badb10da8c1495: Zema? Let's see... That could mean a few things. 'Together', 'people' and 'one' are the ones I remember.
textByHash.7852b842013e2369dba4329dd127f81c0fa961f0: Nothing is my guess. The whole point of Ximoua is that it can't be, and shouldn't be, predicted. It's meant to teach us to live freely and not worry about that which we can't control.
textByHash.0426711e6e1f9648fd41cedba491757f77a8acc2: That, or she herself was so desperate to find a greater meaning in something, that she misinterpreted what she did find.
textByHash.8523b88a9a6bfc916bb8e0c438a13ef8fba8a0ed: I suppose it doesn't matter though. This Final Dawn business is total nonsense, but if the Emissaries believe in it, then we need to work out what they consider so important about this place.
textByHash.104cfc8e364710f00ebbc17d47c96553d2b1cc1a: Indeed. There must be some way to open it...
textByHash.1aa400f9595bda9a92d7056c256ac057174e8818: There must be some way to open it...
textByHash.aae4b2e80b57f13ecdc57c97bc8c4f991973be53: Help me kill this thing, would you?
textByHash.20572fef2db16496920bc18d04e17c8e3dd72ddb: Great work opening the door. Time to see what awaits us on the other side.
textByHash.2b5aa78c0249d39a454fc1cdc748e15738c46f60: On Mokhaiotl. The cultists are trying to get there.
textByHash.3f7e994fbb36cf6f813549a30b7416c48eacb59c: Tlati? As in the rainforest? What do you mean? And what gifts?
textByHash.6b0e2f762450116b76e3bf89eea5b903efda68ac: Ah, there you are. Nilsal, adventurer. I don't believe you've properly met my father. This is Servius, the Teokan of Ralos and the man whose life you saved.
textByHash.5bdc7d4858425050453c1e6f7837b6f8590a9a78: Yes, very fun stuff. All a bit of a convoluted mess as well! I'll be sure to tell you all about it at some point. For now though, , meet Servius. Servius, meet .
textByHash.373ec65b4f22a0fb4a9d72cb36b37f9496ede0ba: Well let's not start celebrating just yet. There's a reason why we're lurking in this crypt.
textByHash.439d9e44e99f079de04cfede0e535dc9b0c1f832: Sorry about that, by the way. I'd have much rather given you a proper welcome on your arrival, but given the situation, it's probably best we take a bit more care.
textByHash.105304c8835e57c0579047f600be06c5f07e7623: Ah, the Tullus twins. Fascinating pair, aren't they?
textByHash.cf70f601223d0a244e8df3b08f61332ee8cf3b28: Oh, you met her! My deepest sympathies.
textByHash.f0ee0555ff91c55cdcfbc65ff71e1898e3f59f86: You're quite right, of course. But anyway, shall we get down to business?
textByHash.37796f58fbb55f90352c65e21206d2e88883a23c: Kuani! So we have quite the predicament on our hands. My mother isn't too happy, oddly enough, and has tasked me with sorting it all out.
textByHash.320e0c627e923b5b324dd870d65093900112c1fd: Someone wants our dear Teokan dead, and we have no idea who! All we know is that they were a Varlamorian.
textByHash.7e936cdfe21f3cf0e70e08b12bb4c1945a185aa3: Right you are! In fact, it limits them to just the members of our little delegation. In other words, myself, Servius, the Tullus twins and our retinue of knights.
textByHash.e246c5be3a8b817e5feb586bc8afb824bac75ae5: Now I don't think it was me... and I'm guessing you didn't try to arrange your own assassination, Servius?
textByHash.6010d5345beb99cd33a01dd83e0b80af7e2c54d8: Too right. Very sloppy work. A top example of how not to assassinate someone. Still, probably best we track them down all the same.
textByHash.fdb718a6dbe5de8e00f9286247ba9c7d9d5e2892: Those knights should also be loyal children of Ralos, but I agree, they are far more likely candidates. Let us investigate them and see what we can find.
textByHash.2c89e2a78f5a46c5414cdcc844f95f15eb546191: Servius, until we have this matter sorted, you should remain here in the capital. The palace will be the safest place.
textByHash.07c3e29a6f6faa3ef5ab8e31adb43cc77442bb38: The Teomat does not offer the protection of the palace. You'll be an easy target there.
textByHash.37995629e019aeafb3e8cdbe4dace4ea8802f728: Well this is a terrible idea that's definitely going to blow up in all our faces later, but it seems you have made your decision. However, I insist that you let me personally accompany you.
textByHash.ef6064aaa6e1b5cfb2998c8b6db4b4c71997421e: , since Servius here seems keen on letting this killer have another go, you'll need to investigate the knights on your own.
textByHash.06eb445ce74ff34a59784ef3b5e447fba708268a: Kuani! Head up to the palace and find the Tullus twins. They dealt with all the fun admin work. They'll know where those knights can be found.
textByHash.328ef2f7d8163a0331197c768565cfa4077b3730: Then it sounds like we're all set. Timoiva, and may the sun light your way!
textByHash.51eb3355a2eacb7cf38fcb7a10142c5194d570d5: Ah, you're back. Shall we get down to business?
textByHash.fdd5003614bf5a20dd73fc076a3e42951f41599f: , head to the palace and find the Tullus twins. They'll know where you can find those knights.
textByHash.061393e7dfb1004bdb208a05320d7cbb0cbbf72b: Well keep at it. The Tullus twins should be able to help you where needed.
textByHash.d24c2b9c4d9fa52cae4137026dd1a5bdee26bbfb: So, how did your investigation go?
textByHash.dc13c19e17f1cdd3fc1553e2e26654026e7738be: Tetamo! One of my own knights! I will need to swiftly deal with this.
textByHash.862198458e6f55a1b55a3eb2b3a754ba607acda0: Ah, excellent! Such should be the fate of all traitors.
textByHash.c90329cde6d06a523a33c4c75d0bb8164d043de8: Hmm, that's unfortunate. It would have been good to know who he was working for. No matter though! I'm sure we'll find another way of getting to the bottom of this.
textByHash.444f19a346bd217a8d8512a0e646940d3e739ac6: Here in Varlamore, justice is delivered swiftly and harshly. Ennius may have taken that a bit too literally... but there's not much we can do about that now.
textByHash.5550d405b104104cff20bf9c1d61cfc186d80084: It's a dangerous accusation indeed. That said... the family does have a bit of a history.
textByHash.b311c89ccc6487c31e1694098bcfe395f4913cc4: My family has not always ruled Varlamore. In fact, it was the Tullus family who reigned for the majority of the kingdom's history.
textByHash.835201111998f1f5c1e31c3cc5fbaea310cfe5a5: That all ended when good old Imafore declared the kingdom to be his new empire. He celebrated by going on a nice little murder spree. One war later and dear old Imafore found himself without a head.
textByHash.0e14a14d42c3f8f56326c2819618c5175358e7c0: Had that not happened, the throne would have eventually passed down to... Furia, I think... I believe she's the slightly older of the two.
textByHash.17a8df765e1157cc37e7ed024a89806fba7c16ae: Yes, very true, but also has valid concerns. We need to be careful of assumptions, but it seems it would be wise for us to keep an eye on the two of them from now on.
textByHash.93e6c64b613cb5764e5c129bef05c9bd2c7884bb: Now, , did your investigation uncover anything else?
textByHash.123b5d8b1320170b5d7214a767fd7dbaf272a16b: Twilight's Promise? I've never heard of that. I wonder if it has anything to do with the Twilight Emissaries...
textByHash.a172f0b5f13ece19ac98c9a8a22d820df7536517: A nice religious cult that has an unhealthy obsession with the end of the world. They've never caused any major problems before, so we've mostly left them be.
textByHash.5e7751b41d87b13fe3f45068d2dcd5353190e4bd: However, given this revelation, perhaps it's time to pay a little closer attention to their dealings.
textByHash.b737997a7be8d860b1c48ff1f9410f73fc9da4c7: As you can see, the Final Dawn is a bit of a... controversial topic. It's a religious belief...
textByHash.10098db11038b5eb7332183c01dc9d80c9123c8f: It's an unofficial religious belief concerning the end of the world. Oddly enough, our friends in the Twilight Emissaries are big fans of it.
textByHash.6c50d55d495b1bf0801585ef014e7a4b05ff61d9: Metzli returned to the Teomat shortly before we did. She'll be around here somewhere.
textByHash.2e5585bf682bb82279271cdfbfae7ce8035cdbbb: If you want to learn about the Final Dawn, Metzli would be the best person to speak to. She'll be around here somewhere.
textByHash.d84c0798123c061da00dd46ab2946718a531b703: I need you outside, now! We have a little bit of an issue!
textByHash.ef4d6b2c5a303aaeb09697b6664b58d94278be64: Yes, of course. Ensure no time is wasted.
textByHash.91d28b2cafa733d72bcf5159b028bd547edbc6df: Well... this has been a bit of a mess.
textByHash.3ba30b1241c7aac5e872f0c95fcf44660a30c240: Word has been sent to mother about what happened here. While we wait to hear back from her, I've already been thinking about a potential plan for how we might go about dealing with our cultist problem.
textByHash.79a72bcd9ad97e5a82db5841027d7b346b11b5e3: However, let's not worry about that just now. Instead, I wanted to thank you for all that you've done. You may be an outsider, but you've already proven yourself to be a true friend to Varlamore. Thank you, iknami.
textByHash.6dc4d17eef13ccdaaf75522f44c5fcf23ce11367: You want to hear it already? I'd have thought you might have wanted a bit of a break from all the excitement, iknami.
textByHash.56d9fefbd9c5d38a3de059f49b253e2f243c9853: I've sent word to mother about what happened here, but I've not heard back yet. Once I have, I'll start planning our next move.
textByHash.7ccee6130f93e2c21e6f1808050729f8edaff56a: Don't worry, I'll be in touch soon enough. Until then, timoiva, and may the sun light your way.
textByHash.d35c1e164df3dfdc649fbeedb700093286df6670: I'm also still yet to hear from mother, but in the meantime, I've been thinking about what our next move should be. I don't have anything solid yet, but I'm sure I will soon.
